reasoning: The Lean statement captures each component of the informal claim precisely: `[MetricSpace X]` gives the metric space structure, `[ConnectedSpace X]` gives connectedness, `∃ x y : X, x ≠ y` is the standard encoding of "more than one point" (i.e., at least two distinct points), and `¬ Countable (univ : Set X)` expresses uncountability of X (via the subtype coercion, this is equivalent to `¬ Countable X`). There are no extra hypotheses (no Fintype, Nonempty, etc.) and no weakening of the conclusion. The formalization is logically equivalent to the informal statement.
